title: Toggl integration with multiple users
excerpt: I had a really solid integration going to Toggl.  Then I hired a staff person.  Now, with two users, all time entries go to the same user in Toggl.  My workaround is to have two timer columns in Monday, two integrations …

posts.author: rgmcpa (Rex McInvale)
posts.cookedHtml:
I had a really solid integration going to Toggl. Then I hired a staff person. Now, with two users, all time entries go to the same user in Toggl. My workaround is to have two timer columns in Monday, two integrations that feed to different default projects in Toggl. It’s gimpy and requires manual effort in Toggl.
Surely there’s a better solution. Can anyone point me in the right direction? TIA!

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey! Just to confirm; are you saying that the same person gets assigned in the Creation Log column? Or in the People Column?

If it’s the same person being assigned in the Creation Log Column, this is to be expected because whoever creates the automation is seen as “doing” the automated action.

Let me know if this is not the case and we can figure out what’s going on here!

posts.author: MichaelTomar (Michael Tomar)
posts.cookedHtml: When integrating multiple users in Toggl with Monday.com, a better solution could be using Skyvia to manage and sync data across users seamlessly.
